feed.title: SpamCrackers — Reference Updates
feed.subtitle: New pillars, tools and standard releases for email-abuse intelligence.

entry.title: Cite & Reproduce — an open dataset catalog
entry.summary: A single hub to cite and independently reproduce every SpamCrackers work — the Standard, the Methodology, the Reference Model, the Patterns Library and the Observatory dataset — with ready citations, exact reproduction steps against public DNS, and a machine-readable catalog.json (CC BY 4.0) that describes every dataset, its version, format and license.

entry.title: Methodology & Limitations, published in full
entry.summary: A single, citable specification of exactly how every grade is produced — data acquisition over DNS-over-HTTPS, the SPF/DKIM/DMARC/MX scoring rubric, the A+ to F scale, the reconciliation between the full check and the Observatory index, what is deliberately out of scope, and the threats to validity that bound every grade. Deterministic and versioned.

entry.title: The Observatory becomes a dated, open record
entry.summary: The Observatory now publishes a weekly, server-measured record of how well-known domains score against the Standard — machine-readable as latest.json (CC BY 4.0) with an immutable weekly archive, and per-domain trend deltas — so email posture can be tracked over time rather than only sampled per visit.

entry.title: The Patterns Library — documented campaign archetypes
entry.summary: Eight defensive campaign archetypes — exact-domain spoofing, AiTM phishing, vendor invoice redirection, executive gift-card fraud, macro-loader ransomware, reply-chain hijack, snowshoe spam and advance-fee fraud — each composing existing techniques into an ordered chain with its earliest point of defence. Published as HTML and machine-readable JSON.

entry.title: The Standard, published as a canonical reference
entry.summary: The SpamCrackers Standard for Email Authentication (v1.0) now has a dedicated, citable page: the four-control rubric, the A+ to F grade scale, methodology and versioning.

entry.title: Machine-readable model published (model.json + JSON Schema)
entry.summary: All 163 techniques, six pillars and the evidence model are now available as structured JSON under CC BY 4.0, with a JSON Schema for validation — a reference layer others can integrate and cite.

entry.title: Glossary — 40 cross-linked terms
entry.summary: Plain-language definitions of 40 email-abuse and authentication terms, each linked to how it is exploited and how to defend against it.

entry.title: The Remediation Playbook
entry.summary: Step-by-step fixes for SPF, DKIM, DMARC and MX, with example records, the safe DMARC rollout, and the abuse each control shuts down.

entry.title: The Observatory — live email-posture index
entry.summary: A live index grading well-known domains against the Standard in the browser, with an inline check for any domain.

entry.title: Email Abuse Intelligence — Reference Model v1.0 complete
entry.summary: The founding umbrella is complete: six active pillars — spam, phishing, BEC, malware, scam and spoofing — with 163 defensively-described techniques, a searchable index, and transparent governance.
